title: ATOM Cloud Infrastructure Sustainability — DAO Funding Pilot
brief: This proposal seeks $2,200 USDC or up to 13,143,639 in ATLAS ($550/month × 4 months) from the DAO Ecosystem fund to keep ATOM Cloud operational through C4. ATOM Cloud currently processes ~100,000 transactions/day for 104 daily active accounts. Users currently pay 0.000005 SOL per taxable transaction (~half of all operations). If this proposal passes, ATOM Cloud becomes completely free for all users until C4. Without funding, the service shuts down in 30 days. This is a pure sustainability request — no new features, no profit of any kind for ATOM Team.
description:
# 1. ATOM Cloud Infrastructure Sustainability — DAO Funding Pilot 

* Author: Sim (Star Atlas community member; ATOM team member)
* Type: Ecosystem fund
* Date: February 2026

___
 
# 2. SUMMARY

This proposal seeks $2,200 USDC or up to 13,143,639 in ATLAS ($550/month × 4 months) from the DAO Ecosystem fund to keep ATOM Cloud operational through C4. ATOM Cloud currently processes ~100,000 transactions/day for 104 daily active accounts. Users currently pay 0.000005 SOL per taxable transaction (~half of all operations). If this proposal passes, ATOM Cloud becomes completely **free for all users** until C4. Without funding, the service shuts down in 30 days. This is a pure sustainability request — no new features, no profit of any kind for ATOM Team.


---
 
# 3. MOTIVATION

ATOM Cloud has been running since September 2024, fully funded by the Naabathon prize — those funds are now exhausted. The platform has maintained 99% uptime, peaked at 190,000+ daily transactions in July 2025 (200+ users during peak), and serves the community at a fixed cost of ~$550 USDC/month. The tool fills a real gap: it lets players automate fleet management and mining without any technical setup, using an intuitive dashboard. Atom-Core, the underlying engine, is open source and free — ATOM Cloud is the hosted layer that makes it accessible to everyone, running 24/7. The ask is simple: cover operational costs for 4 months while the community and the team evaluate whether the platform has long-term legs. 

---

# 4. SPECIFICATION

## 4.1 How ATOM Cloud Works

ATOM Cloud is structured in three layers: API Layer — Queries on-chain fleet and mining state via Star Atlas RPCs on Solana. Scheduler — Monitors fleet state in real-time (Supabase) and dispatches Atom-Core instances to execute player-defined routes automatically. Dashboard — Web interface (Bubble) providing live fleet status, scanning analytics, and account management. Current infrastructure supports a 10x increase in fleet volume with minimal additional cost.


## 4.2 Security 

ATOM Cloud is built on Star Atlas packages and leverages Star Atlas Permissions, developed and maintained by the ATOM team. ATOM creates a new, dedicated hot wallet to which only a limited subset of in-game permissions are delegated — your main wallet and assets remain untouched. The new hot wallet can only sign transactions to use those assets in SAGE, and ONLY in SAGE. While a formal third-party audit has not yet been conducted, the ATOM team has held 2–3 calls with the Star Atlas team and shared official Atom documentation with them. Full technical details: <https://atom-docs.hexon.tools/getting-started/>

## 4.3 What Changes if This Passes

Per-transaction fee removed for all users immediately upon approval. All existing features remain active: mining automation, fleet scheduling, real-time scanning. No new features will be added during the funded period. Monthly public reports published covering: daily active accounts, transaction volume, uptime, costs, and remaining funds. 

## 4.4 End-of-Period Evaluation

At Month 4 (or at C4, whichever comes first), the team will assess transaction volume and community engagement. If usage is strong, a follow-up proposal will be submitted. If interest proves relatively low, the team will evaluate whether continued operation is justified. If C4 is released before Month 4: Payments for any remaining months will cease, and unspent funds will be returned to the treasury. ATOM Cloud will remain operational and free for users through the C4 release, as this represents a natural completion point for the pilot period.


___

# 5. STAKEHOLDER IMPACT

Players: Access to an intuitive cloud automation solution — no technical setup, no fees during the funded period. DAO / Treasury: Bounded, low-cost bet on preserving active community infrastructure. Clear evaluation checkpoint with full transparency. ATOM Cloud Team: No profit. Development and maintenance provided at no cost to the DAO. New Players at C4: Immediate access to automation tools at zero cost. 

---

# 6. IMPLEMENTATION

Payment Structure Amount: $550 USDC/month Duration: 4 months only Total (this proposal): $2,200 USDC or up to 13,143,639 ATLAS 

Schedule: Monthly, paid at the begining of each month
Early termination: Payments cease at C4; remaining funds returned to treasury Receiving Wallet: 8pWEUgcj6XbxRCRnvcHBr5rzy3JHrM5DMQQgPAtZD1V1

 Milestone 1 — Upon Approval Deliverables: 
*  ATOM Cloud confirmed operational
*  Per-transaction fee removed
*  Payment: $550 USDC released upon Council verification.

Milestone 2 — Months 2–4 Continuous Operation Deliverables: 
* ATOM Cloud remains operational with 99%+ uptime
* Per-transaction fee remains disabled
* Monthly public reports published covering: daily active accounts, transaction volume, uptime, costs, and remaining funds
* Payment: $550 USDC or the remaining atlas (total max of between milestone 1 and 2 of 13,143,639 atlas) released at the beginning of each Month, contingent on Milestone 1 completion and ATOM Cloud being operational.



___

# 7. COST & RESOURCE ALLOCATION

Monthly Budget Breakdown: 

Bubble (Dashboard hosting): ~$143 USDC Hetzner (GDPR-compliant server): ~$255 USDC Supabase (Real-time database): $110 USDC Betterstack (Logging): $34 USDC Buffer (exchange rate): ~$8 USDC Total: ~$550 USDC/month No profit margin. If infrastructure scaling is ever required due to user growth, any additional cost will be requested transparently in a separate proposal. 

---
# 8. ADDITIONAL DETAILS

Conflicts of Interest: There is no financial profit involved — this proposal covers infrastructure costs only. Monthly expense reports are available upon request to ensure full transparency. The one and only interest here is to keep alive a tools which was used by more than 20% of total player base during the peak, and still being used by more than 15% of the current playerbase. 

Earlier Versions: This is the first formal PIP draft. Prior community discussion occurred in the temperature check phase as required by the governance process. Transparency Note: Monthly expense reports are available upon request. Any unspent funds at the end of the funded period (whether at Month 4 or at C4 release) will be returned to the DAO treasury.
